summary: Amber Wall is a new tank plant for Rootline Defense — a tile-holding wall that soaks sniper bolts and screens attackers instead of adding damage. Today's Hold the Line board is tuned so the previous roster can no longer clear: the wall is what buys time against Briar Snipers and Glass Rams, and it makes the canonical winning line readable before endless unlocks.

candidates.title: Amber Wall — First Defender Role Contract and the Hold the Line Board
candidates.summary: Adds Amber Wall as a high-health, non-attacking defender, ships the reusable `role: "defender"` plant contract, updates Board Scout with the wall's role and detail rows, and authors the April 20 Hold the Line board where the old roster fails and the wall-enabled roster clears.

candidates.title: Board Proof — Verified Clear Replay for Today's Challenge
candidates.summary: Expose a public, optional replay that proves today's board is winnable and teaches the canonical clear line before a player starts a run.

candidates.title: Damage-State Readouts — Wall Health Cracks and Break Alerts
candidates.summary: Focus the day entirely on higher-contrast damage feedback for defenders so players can read when a wall is holding, failing, and worth replacing.

winner.title: Amber Wall — First Defender Role Contract and the Hold the Line Board
winner.summary: Adds Amber Wall as Rootline Defense's first defender plant, formalizes the reusable defender-role contract, and ships the Hold the Line board where the wall is required to clear.

rationale: Amber Wall won because it adds the clearest missing lane-defense verb in the current roster: a true blocker that buys time instead of simply adding more damage. It compounds cleanly through a reusable defender-role contract, pays off immediately in the Hold the Line scenario, and gives Board Scout, tutorial flow, challenge gating, and endless unlocks a stronger shared language.

feedbackInfluence.summary: Players wanted a true blocker plant that buys time under sniper and ram pressure instead of solving every lane with more DPS.
Board Scout feedback pushed new mechanics into pre-run copy so the wall's role and lane purpose are visible before a challenge starts.

bluesky_post.headline: Amber Wall — the plant that just holds the line
bluesky_post.body: Rootline Defense ships its first defender: 120 HP, no attack. Screens sniper bolts, anchors siege lanes, forces real composition choices. Today's 'Hold the Line' board can't be cleared without it.
bluesky_post.notes: Today's post ships text-only — no screenshot attached. If a screenshot is captured later, use alt_text: 'Board Scout card for Amber Wall beside the Hold the Line board, showing a chunky amber-tinted lane wall screening a Thorn Vine from an incoming crimson sniper bolt.'

replyTemplates.whatIs: Amber Wall is Rootline Defense's first dedicated defender plant — 120 HP, no attack. Its whole job is to hold a tile and soak sniper bolts so the plants behind it keep firing.
replyTemplates.howDoesItWork: Drop a wall in a sniper's lane between the sniper and your Sunroot/Bramble Spear. The sniper retargets the wall, the wall soaks bolts until it breaks, and the wall also counts toward Glass Ram's required-defenders rule.
replyTemplates.genericQuestion: Today's 'Hold the Line' board is tuned so the previous roster can't clear — Amber Wall is the key. Try it at commandgarden.com.
